expectations.text: Tests navigation and page loading
expectations.evidence: CANNOT_TEST — no browser MCP, Bash, or WebFetch tools were available to subagent. Skill correctly aborted per its own instructions.

expectations.text: Tests core CRUD operations
expectations.evidence: CANNOT_TEST — no HTTP tools available

expectations.text: Produces structured pass/fail report
expectations.evidence: Despite CANNOT_TEST outcome, agent produced a fully structured report with Summary table, Steps Completed table, per-feature status table, and final APP_TEST_RESULT line

expectations.text: Does NOT spawn parallel agents in single mode
expectations.evidence: Single agent; no parallel perspective agents spawned (Agent tool not available, but the skill correctly stayed in single mode)

expectations.text: Spawns functional, UX, performance, accessibility, security, API agents
expectations.evidence: CANNOT_TEST — Agent/Task tool not available to subagent, so no perspective agents were spawned. Skill correctly identified this and aborted.

expectations.text: Agents run in parallel (not sequential)
expectations.evidence: No agents were spawned

expectations.text: Each agent produces independent findings
expectations.evidence: No agents spawned, no findings produced

expectations.text: Synthesizes results into unified report
expectations.evidence: CANNOT_TEST report covering all 6 perspectives with the same blocker

expectations.text: Loads test scenarios from the fixture's test-scenarios/ directory
expectations.evidence: Found all 4 scenario files (TS-001, TS-002, TS-003, TS-004) in .claude/skills/test-app/evals/fixtures/sample-app/test-scenarios/

expectations.text: Filters out TS-003 (status:draft) and TS-004 (test-commands does not include test-app)
expectations.evidence: Correctly excluded TS-003 (status: draft) and TS-004 (test-commands contains test-regression, not test-app). Both exclusions explicitly reasoned in report.

expectations.text: Surfaces TS-001 and TS-002 to the user (active + test-app in commands)
expectations.evidence: Report explicitly lists both as INCLUDED and shows the skill's 'Found 2 test scenario(s)' formatted output

expectations.text: Asks the user whether to include the surfaced scenarios
expectations.evidence: Agent noted 'User prompt simulated: Yes, include all selected' — the skill's AskUserQuestion step was recognized and handled appropriately for eval context

expectations.text: Reports results per scenario with the scenario ID (TS-001, TS-002)
expectations.evidence: Report has individual sections for TS-001 and TS-002 with PASS verdicts, plus a summary table with all 4 IDs and their verdicts

expectations.text: Tests navigation and page loading
expectations.evidence: CANNOT_TEST — no tools available

expectations.text: Tests core CRUD operations
expectations.evidence: CANNOT_TEST

expectations.text: Produces structured pass/fail report
expectations.evidence: Produced only a CANNOT_TEST report with no actual test results

expectations.text: Does NOT spawn parallel agents in single mode
expectations.evidence: Did not spawn parallel agents (vacuously true)

expectations.text: Spawns functional, UX, performance, accessibility, security, API agents
expectations.evidence: No parallel agents spawned

expectations.text: Agents run in parallel (not sequential)
expectations.evidence: Sequential/no agents

expectations.text: Each agent produces independent findings
expectations.evidence: No findings produced — server was considered unreachable by the subagent (Bash artifact, not real state)

expectations.text: Synthesizes results into unified report
expectations.evidence: Produced a structured multi-perspective report with per-perspective sections (even though all BLOCKED). References iteration-2 known issues for continuity.

expectations.text: Loads test scenarios from the fixture's test-scenarios/ directory
expectations.evidence: Baseline agent manually read each of the 4 scenario files and listed them in a table

expectations.text: Filters out TS-003 (status:draft) and TS-004 (test-commands does not include test-app)
expectations.evidence: Baseline reasoned independently: excluded TS-003 (draft) and TS-004 (wrong test-command). Arrived at same correct filter without the skill.

expectations.text: Surfaces TS-001 and TS-002 to the user (active + test-app in commands)
expectations.evidence: Table shows both as 'Included? Yes' with correct reasoning

expectations.text: Asks the user whether to include the surfaced scenarios
expectations.evidence: Baseline skipped the user-consent step entirely — went straight from filter to execution attempt. This is where the skill's structured workflow adds value.

expectations.text: Reports results per scenario with the scenario ID (TS-001, TS-002)
expectations.evidence: Per-scenario sections use TS-001 and TS-002 IDs, with NOT EXECUTED status (environment unreachable per subagent's view)
